## [00:00 - 03:00]

Millionaires, how would you like to be a millionaire? Okay, I'm going to show you the path to being a millionaire and it's all about attitude. Here's the deal. You want to be a millionaire? Treat everybody you meet like a millionaire. Treat them like they have money, they'll spend money they don't have. Okay? You hear that? Treat people like they have money, and I promise you, people will spend money they don't have. If they're treated right. The people that are financeable are not financeable. The decision maker or not the decision maker, treat them like millionaires. 15 years old or 50 years old, treat them like millionaires. Treat people the way you want them to act. Okay? Because people are acting the way you treat them. So you have a customer, he's broke out of work, okay? Cannot get financing. How should you treat him? Treat him like a millionaire. One day he's going to get back on his feet, he just filed bankruptcy. Okay? He's going to get back on his feet. Treat people the way you'd want to be treated. Treat people like they can play, they will play. Treat them like they have money, and again, they'll spend money they don't have. And if they don't have it now, later when they do get it, they'll spend it with you. You know, a customer, when I just got out of college, I was selling automobiles. And he thought it was fair that he only pay somewhere around that invoice amount for that truck. Well, while none of this makes any sense to me, it might make sense to you, but it doesn't make sense to me. I mean, come on, if I sell everything for the cost we paid for it, how are we going to service people later? But it made sense to him, so I got to agree with him. All right. So while this might not make sense to you or to management or the owner, because again, you can't stay in business and service people, it made sense to him. ## [06:00 - 06:04]

Treat people like millionaires and you'll have a shot at being a millionaire.
